summary: Conjecture 4.1 from arXiv:2404.02021 asks whether every Ramsey coloring of order N for 3-graphs G,H with pair-construction complexity p > r(G,H)^{o(1)} must have complexity at least r(G,H)^a for an absolute constant a>0. No follow-up paper confirming or refuting this conjecture was found. A related 2026 preprint by He (arXiv:2603.16069) constructs 3-graphs with quasipolynomial Ramsey growth rates in the same research area, but its abstract does not address the pair-construction complexity framework of Conjecture 4.1.

internal_refs_verified.note: This paper (Nguyen–Scott–Seymour, 'Induced subgraph density. VI. Bounded VC-dimension') confirms a conjecture of Fox, Pach, and Suk about cliques/stable sets in graphs of bounded VC-dimension. It is unrelated to Conjecture 4.1 from arXiv:2404.02021, which concerns pair constructions of complexity p for 3-uniform hypergraph Ramsey numbers. The internal reference is a false match.

search_queries: hypergraph Ramsey pair construction complexity stepping-up Conlon Fox Gunby He Mubayi Suk 2024
arxiv 2404.02021 cite pair construction r^p hypergraph Ramsey complexity 2024 2025
notes: The conjecture is a structural/framework-level claim asserting that pair constructions of low complexity are essentially universal for hypergraph Ramsey lower bounds. The source paper was published in IMRN (Volume 2025, Issue 11). A 2026 preprint arXiv:2603.16069 by He (a co-author) constructs 3-graphs with quasipolynomial Ramsey growth rates n^{Theta(log n)}, directly relevant to the surrounding discussion, but could not be confirmed to address Conjecture 4.1 specifically within the 5-call budget.
